description_long: This house features a classic rectangular brick structure with a steeply pitched gable roof. The roof is covered with overlapping shingles and includes several skylights, as seen from the top-down views. The brick walls are solid and uniform, with no visible ornamentation or protrusions. The roof framing is visible in some views, revealing wooden rafters and cross-bracing, suggesting ongoing construction or a partially transparent roof for visualization. The windows are arranged in a regular pattern along the roof, providing natural light to the upper interior. The overall design is simple and functional, typical of suburban or rural residential buildings.
description: A rectangular brick house with a steep gable shingle roof and regularly spaced skylights, featuring visible wooden roof framing.
description_view_0: End view of the house showing a solid brick wall and the full height of the gable roof.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the wooden roof framing, cross-bracing, and transparent roof sections with skylights.
description_view_2: Opposite end view, similar to view 0, with a solid brick wall and gable roof.
description_view_3: Side view with visible roof framing and three skylights set into the roof structure.
attribute_window_arrangement: symmetrical rows of skylights on the roof
attribute_entryway_design: Simple main entryway, likely a single door with no visible porch or decorative elements
